DECLARATION OF MELINDA DERISH IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO QUASH OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE ISSUE A PROTECTIVE ORDER FILED BY PLAINTIFF LINEHAN, DIANE INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GUAARDIAN AD LITEM FOR SIOBHAN LINEHAN, A MINOR, PATRICK LINEHAN, A MINOR AND MOLLY LINEHAN, A MINOR, THROUGH THEIR GUARDIAN AD LITEM DIANE LINEHAN

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST MARY'S MEDICAL'S MOTION TO QUASH OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE ISSUE A PROTECTIVE ORDER REGARDING THE NOTICE OF TAKING A VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS OF HELENA LIM. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED AND ALTERNATE REQUEST FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE PROSPECTIVE DEPONENT IS SERIOUSLY ILL. HOWEVER, DEPONENT IS POTENTIALLY IN POSSESSION OF INFORMATION ESSENTIAL TO THE TRIAL OF THIS CASE ABOUT THE AMOUNT OF DILAUDID ADMINISTERED TO PLAINTIFF WHICH IS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF DEATH. DEPONENT WAS THE DIRECTOR OF PHARMACY AT THE TIME THE PHARMACY WAS ASKED FOR THE RE-CREATION OF PLAINTIFF'S MEDICAL RECORDS AND WAS THE ONLY ST. MARY'S EMPLOYEE TO PROVIDE INFORMATION TO THE S.F. MEDICAL EXAMINER ABOUT THE TIMING, DOSAGE, AND CONCENTRATION OF DILAUDID. DEPONENT'S DEPOSITION WAS FIRST SOUGHT ON MAY 9, 2012. DEPONENT WAS ABLE TO PROVIDE A DETAILED DECLARATION IN OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION. THE DECLARATION SUGGESTS THAT THE DEPOSITION OF MISS LIM WILL NOT BE LENGTHY, GIVEN HER LIMITED INVOLVEMENT AND HER LACK OF "SPECIFIC MEMORY" AS TO CERTAIN EVENTS. UNDER ALL OF THE CIRCUMSTANCES, PLAINTIFF SHALL BE PERMITTED TO TAKE THE DEPOSITION, ASK QUESTIONS AND CROSS-EXAMINE HER ON THE DECLARATION. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER ON TAKING THE DEPOSITION AT A PLACE AND TIME THAT IS THE LEAST BURDENSOME ON THE DEPONENT, INCLUDING AT HER HOME IF DEPONENT SO REQUESTS, WITH AS MANY BREAKS AS NECESSARY. THE TOTAL TIME OF DEPOSITION QUESTIONING SHALL NOT LAST FOR MORE THAN ONE HOUR. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: TAMRA KEENE, CSR # 5404

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash In The Alt Issue A Protective Order Regarding The Ntc Of Taking A Videotaped Deposition And Production Of Documents Of Helena Lim
Off Calendar Jan-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Elizabeth Ryan, Esq. of Bjork Lawrence, Tel. 925.279.1150, for defendant and moving party;
Melinda T. Derish, Esq. of Walkup Melodia Kelly Wecht & Schoenberger, Tel. 415.981.7210, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST MARY'S MEDICAL'S MOTION To Quash or in the Alternative Issue A Protective Order Regarding The Notice Of Taking A Videotaped Deposition And Production Of Documents Of Helena Lim. The motion to quash is denied and alternate request for protective order is granted in part. It is undisputed that the prospective deponent is seriously ill. However, deponent is potentially in possession of information essential to the trial of this case about the amount of Dilaudid administered to Plaintiff which is reasonably calculated to lead to admissible evidence regarding Plaintiff's cause of death. Deponent was the director of pharmacy at the time the pharmacy was asked for the re-creation of Plaintiff's medical records and was the only St. Mary's employee to provide information to the S.F. Medical Examiner about the timing, dosage, and concentration of Dilaudid. Deponent's deposition was first sought on May 9, 2012. Deponent was able to provide a detailed declaration in opposition to this motion. The declaration suggests that the deposition of Miss Lim will not be lengthy, given her limited involvement and her lack of "specific memory" as to certain events. Under all of the circumstances, Plaintiff shall be permitted to take the deposition, ask questions and cross-examine her on the declaration. The parties shall meet and confer on taking the deposition at a place and time that is the least burdensome on the Deponent, including at her home if Deponent so requests, with as many breaks as necessary. The total time of deposition questioning shall not last for more than one hour. Sanctions are denied. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Tamra Keene, CSR # 5404
